advantages: Captures smooth curvature with a small, interpretable set of coefficients—easy to explain and deploy.
Reuses the entire linear regression toolbox (OLS, ridge, lasso, cross-validation) after feature expansion.
Closed-form or convex solutions remain available; no fragile non-linear optimization loop for the base case.
Fast inference: prediction is a dot product of stored coefficients with a few polynomial terms.
disadvantages: High degrees overfit easily, producing oscillations between training points (Runge-style behavior).
Feature count grows combinatorially with degree and number of inputs when interactions are included.
Extrapolation beyond the training x-range is unreliable—polynomials diverge quickly outside observed data.
Sensitive to outliers and ill-conditioned design matrices when powers are computed on unscaled inputs.
